pairs.question: Who was Raja Ajit Singh Bahadur and which estate did he rule?
pairs.answer: Raja Ajit Singh Bahadur was the ruler of the Shekhawat estate (thikana) of Khetri at Panchpana in Rajasthan from 1870 to 1901. He was born on 16 October 1861 at Alsisar and became the eighth king of Khetri after the death of Fateh Singh.

pairs.question: How did Ajit Singh become the ruler of Khetri?
pairs.answer: Ajit Singh was adopted to Khetri and became the eighth king of Khetri in 1870 following the death of Fateh Singh.

pairs.question: What role did Ajit Singh play in Swami Vivekananda's life?
pairs.answer: Ajit Singh was a close friend and disciple of Swami Vivekananda. He suggested the name 'Vivekananda' to him, provided financial support starting in 1891, and encouraged him to speak at the Parliament of the World's Religions in Chicago in 1893.

pairs.question: Describe the financial support Ajit Singh provided to Swami Vivekananda's family.
pairs.answer: From 1891, Ajit Singh sent a monthly stipend of ₹100 to Vivekananda's family in Kolkata. In 1898, Vivekananda wrote a letter requesting him to make the donation permanent so that his mother, Bhuvaneswari Devi, would continue receiving financial assistance after his death.

pairs.question: What is known about Ajit Singh's family life?
pairs.answer: Ajit Singh married Rani Champawatiji Sahiba in 1876. The couple had one son and two daughters. His father was Thakur Chattu Singh, a resident of Alsisar.

pairs.question: Where and when did Ajit Singh die?
pairs.answer: Ajit Singh died on 18 January 1901 due to an accident at the tomb of Akbar in Sikandra near Agra. He was later cremated at Mathura.

pairs.question: How many times did Swami Vivekananda meet Ajit Singh, and in which years?
pairs.answer: Swami Vivekananda met Ajit Singh three times in his lifetime—in 1891, 1893, and 1897.
